title: The Honey Scandal Proves Cookie Attribution Is Broken
summary: MegaLag exposed PayPal’s Honey overwriting affiliate cookies at checkout. 25+ lawsuits followed. Here’s what it means for attribution.

title: Why Your Ad Attribution Numbers Are Wrong
summary: Platform-reported metrics inflate conversions by 30–50%. Here’s why the numbers don’t add up and what independent measurement looks like.

title: What Is Cryptographic Ad Attribution?
summary: Signed tokens instead of cookies. The advertiser issues, the customer carries, the advertiser verifies. No platform dashboard needed.

title: Cookie Hijacking: What Advertisers and Publishers Should Know
summary: Honey wasn’t alone. Capital One Shopping and Rakuten did the same thing. The cookie is the vulnerability, not the extension.

title: The Forbes MFA Scandal and the $770M Programmatic Waste Problem
summary: Forbes ran a secret subdomain as a content farm. $770M per quarter goes to MFA sites. Coupons that need real customers expose the fakes.

title: How Publishers Can Prove Their Inventory Converts
summary: A niche podcast at 12% conversion beats a content farm at 0.01%. Cryptographic coupons give publishers the receipts to prove it.

title: Meta’s $3 Billion Scam Ad Problem
summary: Internal docs show 19% of Meta’s China ad revenue came from scams. They had an anti-fraud team. They disbanded it.

title: Creators Are Organizing. Attribution Is Why.
summary: Creator ad spend hit $37B but 57% of marketers can’t track ROI. SAG-AFTRA is organizing creators. The real issue is provability.

title: Google’s Ad Monopoly Ruling: What Changes for Advertisers
summary: Google lost its second antitrust case. Divestiture of AdX and DFP may follow. What this means for measurement independence.

title: The Ad Verification Industry Missed the Bots
summary: DoubleVerify missed 21% of bots. IAS labeled bot traffic as human 77% of the time. Verification asks the wrong question.

title: After Third-Party Cookies: Attribution Without Tracking
summary: Every cookie replacement recreates the same problems. Bearer attribution never depended on cookies in the first place.
